  *MultiStream* are **closed-loop** ("LoadGen sends next query as soon as SUT
  completes the previous query"; metrics = 90th- resp. 99th-percentile
  early-stopping latency estimate), *Server* is **open-loop** (LoadGen issues
  queries on a scheduled Poisson arrival process; metric = maximum Poisson
  throughput parameter sustainable under the latency bound), and *Offline*
  sends all samples at once (metric = throughput). Minimum run duration
  **600 seconds** in every scenario. So MLPerf itself uses closed-loop drivers
  where the claim is isolated per-query service time and an open-loop driver
  where the claim is latency under offered load — a distinction §5 builds on.
- **Query-count statistics are explicit, not vibes — but they are guidance,
  and duration always binds**: the rules' worked example computes that
  estimating a 90th percentile at 99% confidence with a 0.5% margin takes
  **23,886 inferences**, and the minimum-query table built on such numbers is
  offered as "a suggested starting point for how to set the minimum number of
  queries" — NOT a universal required sample size. The early-stopping
  criterion then allows runs with fewer processed queries to remain valid at
  the penalty of a slightly higher effective percentile, and it applies only
  ONCE THE MINIMUM RUN DURATION IS MET (its algorithm's step 1 begins "When
  the minimum run duration is met…") — early stopping relaxes query counts,
  never the 600-s duration [LIT-BACKED: inference_rules.adoc, scenarios table
  + early-stopping appendix]. Higher percentiles need correspondingly more
  queries — this is the reason programme-3 pinned p50/p95 rather than p99 for
  per-query latency (§1.3(f)), and it is the right call at our suite sizes
  [STIPULATED, arithmetic from the rules' own confidence machinery].
- **LLM serving constraints are TTFT/TPOT pairs per benchmark**, e.g.
  Llama3.1-8B summarization: TTFT 2000 ms / TPOT 100 ms (conversational) and
  500 ms / 30 ms (interactive); Llama2-70B: 2000/200 and 450/40; Mixtral-8x7B
  2000/200 [LIT-BACKED: inference_rules.adoc]. The percentile at which the LLM
  TTFT/TPOT constraints bind was not shown in the fetched excerpt —
  **UNVERIFIED** (commonly stated as 99th; do not repeat without checking the
  per-benchmark table at freeze time).
- **Caching rules** (directly relevant to our warm/cold accounting): caching
  queries, responses, or "values derived from activations" is prohibited; KV
  cache is allowed only when reused within the batch of queries; the trace may
  be pinned [LIT-BACKED: inference_rules.adoc]. MLPerf measures a warmed
  steady state — there is **no cold-start scenario in MLPerf at all**; our
  KOT-COST/2 clause (h) (warm AND cold both measured) is beyond-MLPerf and has
  to be designed by P3-D-HW, not copied (§6).
- **Scale gap, stated plainly**: the smallest LLM workload in the current
  suite is Llama3.1-8B (edge, summarization) [LIT-BACKED:
  inference_rules.adoc]; there is no MLPerf artifact at 100M–2B. What
  transfers to Programme-3 is the *machinery* — scenario definitions,
  percentile-with-confidence early stopping, minimum durations, seeded LoadGen
  trace generation, caching prohibitions — not any workload [STIPULATED].

DECISION for P3-D-HW [STIPULATED]: adopt (a) the SingleStream and Offline
scenarios as our two mandatory load shapes — SingleStream's closed-loop driver
yields isolated per-query **service-time** percentiles and every such figure
is labelled as such; Offline yields batch throughput — with a Server/Poisson
open-loop scenario MANDATORY for any latency-under-offered-load claim and
otherwise optional per rung under the pinned concurrency distribution already
required by KOT-COST/2(g); (b) the 600-second minimum-duration rule
unconditionally, with the early-stopping machinery used only to size/trim
query counts above that duration (never as a substitute for it); (c) a
query-count-per-percentile table computed from the same confidence machinery
at our n; (d) MLPerf's caching prohibitions as the default legality rule for
comparator arms (a comparator may not memoise the test trace — this closes a
gaming channel the threat model P3-D-THREAT should name).

## 2. MLPerf Power: what the methodology is, and what we can honestly claim

The power methodology was verified at the paper [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2410.12032,
"MLPerf Power" (Tschand et al.), v2 2025]:

- **Full-system wall power is the tenet**: "a fundamental tenet of MLPerf
  Power is the emphasis on measuring full system power consumption" — via
  SPEC-certified external analyzers (e.g. Yokogawa WT310) driven through the
  PTDaemon, with a **range-fixing calibration run** before measurement runs, a
  minimum of **60 seconds of power samples**, energy computed by integrating
  power over the run window, and efficiency reported as Samples/Joule.
  Datacenter measurements include compute nodes + interconnect fabric; cooling
  and storage nodes are explicitly not yet covered. 1,841 reproducible
  measurements across 60 systems, µW→MW.
- The paper also kills two folk practices we must not reintroduce: TDP as a
  power proxy ("TDP only represents the thermal design limit, not the actual
  power usage") and cloud-granularity measurements that sweep in idle or
  unrelated nodes [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2410.12032].

**The honest consequence for us** [STIPULATED, load-bearing]: we have no wall
meter and no PTDaemon on any pool platform surveyed so far — the local EC2 VM
and Modal containers are virtualized/containerized, and whether ARC offers a
bare-metal node with any power-measurement path is exactly the open survey
item §11 hands P3-D-HW (it is not settled either way here). **On the pool as
currently known, no Programme-3 energy figure can be labeled
MLPerf-Power-compliant.** What we CAN adopt is the
discipline around the number: fixed measurement boundary declared per figure,
calibration/ranging run, minimum measurement duration, whole-run integration
(already required by KOT-COST/2(d)), and Samples/Joule-style normalisation.
Our energy figures are **counter-based component estimates** (GPU via NVML
energy counters; CPU energy unavailable on the measured local box and
presumptively on other virtualized hosts pending the per-host survey, §3) and
must be
reported with that label, fail-closed: where no counter exists, the energy
cell of the resource vector is reported MISSING, never modeled from TDP or
utilisation [STIPULATED — this is the §1.3 no-silent-fallback convention
applied to the rig].

**Proposed W1 admissibility rule for energy (the review's blocking question,
answered)** [STIPULATED — proposed in-doc for the coordinator to carry into
`registry/assumptions.jsonl`; nothing is registered by this deliverable]: a
MISSING cell is honest reporting but does not by itself define how arms with
different or absent energy measurements participate in the binding W1
resource frontier. GPU-only NVML counters, CPU RAPL, and full-system wall
power are DIFFERENT measurement boundaries and are never directly comparable.
Therefore: (i) an energy **comparison** between arms is admissible only when
every compared arm carries a measurement at the SAME declared boundary, from
the same instrument class, on the same rig/session; (ii) no cross-boundary
conversion (e.g. GPU-counter → full-system) may be used unless a validated
conversion model with quantified error is itself pre-registered — none exists
today; (iii) consequently the energy component of the §1.3 resource vector is
**non-binding by default in W1**: it is always reported per arm with its
boundary label (or MISSING), and it becomes a binding frontier dimension only
on rigs where (i) holds for ALL compared arms. A MISSING or
boundary-mismatched cell excludes the energy dimension from that comparison
(disclosed in the verdict); it does not disqualify the arm. P3-D-HW inherits
this as its default and may strengthen it (e.g. by finding a same-boundary
path on ARC), never weaken it silently.

## 3. RAPL: pitfalls, and the measured fact that we don't have it

- **Accuracy when it exists**: the standard reference (Khan et al., "RAPL in
  Action", ACM TOMPECS 2018) finds RAPL readings highly correlated with plug
  power with negligible read overhead; accuracy improved generationally —
  Sandy Bridge-EP shows systematic errors while Haswell-EP correlates
  near-perfectly with external measurement (reported MAPE ~4.0% vs ~3.1%
  without the DRAM domain) [LIT-BACKED: TOMPECS 3(2) 2018, abstract + Aalto
  actually the median number of reported system parameters)].
- MLPerf, by contrast, standardises only the warmed steady state (§1) and
  prohibits response caching; **no public ML benchmark standardises cold-start
  + index-load accounting** — the closest formalisation is our own KOT-SIZE/2
  figure (4) (cold-start working set + total bytes read to first answer)
  [STIPULATED after search; carried as an absence-claim, i.e. no
  counter-example found this session, not a proven negative].
- DECISION for P3-D-HW [STIPULATED]: define three pinned states per arm —
  **COLD** (fresh container/process, page cache dropped
  (`echo 3 > drop_caches` where privileged, else fresh-VM/container),
  model+index load INCLUDED in the measured window; yields startup time and
  bytes-to-first-answer), **WARM** (steady state after a pinned warmup trace,
  MLPerf-style; yields the p50/p95/throughput figures), and **REPORT BOTH**,
  never a blend. The warmup trace and its length are pinned inputs; cold-run
  repetition count is CI-determined per Traeger's actual rule (repeat until
  the §7 rank-based CI half-width gate passes), with a stipulated minimum of
  10 — OUR configuration, echoing Traeger's own Auto-pilot practice rather
  than any literature-prescribed floor; cold-path variance is I/O-dominated,
  so expect the gate, not the minimum, to bind.
- Arithmetic floor worth pinning [EXTRAPOLATION, from verified baseline
  throughput]: an EBS gp3 volume's included baseline is 125 MB/s (§8), so
  every GB of model+index bytes costs ≥8 s of cold-start on baseline gp3
  before compute begins — cold-start economics are bytes economics, which is
  exactly why KOT-SIZE/2(4) and KOT-COST/2(h) must share one measurement.

## 7. Hardware repeatability: run-to-run and day-to-day variance control

- **The reporting canon** is Hoefler & Belli, "Scientific Benchmarking of
  Parallel Computing Systems" (SC15) — 12 rules, verified at the primary PDF
  this session. The ones that bind our rig [LIT-BACKED: SC15, 2015]:
  Rule 3/4 (arithmetic mean only for costs, harmonic for rates; avoid
  summarizing ratios — summarize costs/rates instead, geometric mean only as
  last resort); Rule 5 (report if measurements are deterministic; otherwise
  report confidence intervals); Rule 6 (do NOT assume normality without
  diagnostic checking); Rule 7 (compare nondeterministic data via
  non-overlapping CIs / ANOVA); Rule 8 (means/medians may be the wrong
  summary — worst-case-latency problems need percentiles); Rule 9 (document
  ALL varying factors and levels). For medians/percentiles they give the
  distribution-free rank-based CI (binomial ranks around n/2), which needs no
  normality assumption, and recommend CoV (s/x̄) as a consistency measure.
- **Measurement bias is real and large at our effect sizes**: Mytkowicz et
  al., "Producing Wrong Data Without Doing Anything Obviously Wrong!" (ASPLOS
  2009) showed UNIX environment size and link order alone shift measured
  performance by >10% (double-digit percent in cases), and prescribe
  randomizing the experimental setup across runs plus diverse workloads
  [LIT-BACKED: ASPLOS 2009, primary PDF]. Our W1 margins δ_k will plausibly be
  single-digit index points riding on cost measurements; a 10% systematic
  wobble in the cost denominator is disqualifying if uncontrolled
  [STIPULATED].
- **Cloud/day-to-day variance**: our pool is virtualized and shared (EC2 dev
  box with 2 shared cores; Modal serverless containers land on unknown hosts;
  ARC is a shared cluster). MLPerf Power's warning about cloud measurement
  granularity (§2) applies to timing too. DECISION for P3-D-HW [STIPULATED]:
  (a) same-host pairing — every S-vs-comparator cost comparison runs
  interleaved in the SAME session on the SAME container/node (A/B/A/B), never
  across days or hosts; (b) host fingerprint recorded per run (CPU model,
  GPU UUID, driver, clocks, container image hash) — the pinned-rig rule
  `06-resources.md:86` extended to identity; (c) per-session calibration
  probe: a fixed synthetic workload run at session start/end; sessions whose
  probe CoV exceeds a pinned gate (suggest 5% to start; calibrate in
  P3-E-CAL) are discarded fail-closed; (d) repetitions per cell driven by a
  pinned CI gate — rank-based nonparametric CIs per Hoefler Rule 6/8, run
  count increased until the half-width gate passes (Traeger's actual rule:
  the number of runs is determined from confidence levels) — with a
  stipulated minimum of 10 reps as our starting configuration, not a
  literature-backed floor; (e)
  environment pinned AND a randomized-dummy-env-var replicate per Mytkowicz to
  bound layout bias on CPU-side measurements.

## 8. Storage + network I/O economics

**Price points for OUR pool, verified 2026-07-11** (prices drift; these are
pins for Phase-1 modelling, to be refreshed at P3-D-LIFE freeze):

- **Modal** (the primary GPU home) [LIT-BACKED: modal.com/pricing]: H100
  $0.001097/s (≈$3.95/h); A100-80GB $0.000694/s (≈$2.50/h); A100-40GB
  $0.000583/s (≈$2.10/h); L40S ≈$1.95/h; A10 ≈$1.10/h; T4 ≈$0.59/h; L4
  ≈$0.80/h; CPU $0.0000131/core-s (≈$0.047/core-h); RAM $0.00000222/GiB-s
  (≈$0.008/GiB-h); Volumes $0.09/GiB-month with 1 TiB free; academic credits
  up to $10k (already in the §6 funding plan).
- **AWS (us-east-1)** [LIT-BACKED: aws.amazon.com pricing pages, partially
  "inference-optimal" — no specified target quality, workload, hardware,
  lifetime demand, or cost model makes SmolLM2 a solution to Sardana's
  optimisation, and HOW MUCH of the available train-longer-deploy-smaller
  gain these artifacts bank is unquantified. The claim this review needs is
  weaker and holds: our baseline frontier is built from inference-lean,
  deliberately overtrained artifacts rather than compute-optimal ones, so W1
  margins cannot be manufactured by comparing against compute-optimal
  strawmen [STIPULATED, load-bearing for P3-D-BASE/P3-D-LIFE].
- **Accounting consequence for KOT-LIFE/1** [STIPULATED]: the ledger's three
  pinned volumes (10³/10⁶/10⁹ queries) should be joined by Sardana-style
  crossover statements per claim — computed **per priced dimension, never on
  the vector**: a resource VECTOR has no crossover point unless its
  components are priced or constrained on a common scale, and this design
  deliberately refuses a universal exchange rate across human hours, GPU
  hours, latency, and energy (§1.3). So: report, separately for each
  dimension that has a defensible price (at minimum the $-denominated ones),
  the inference volume at which the measured per-query gap between S and
  each comparator repays any training/authoring cost difference — each line
  under stated billing-granularity/utilisation/horizon assumptions with
  sensitivity analysis (§11 P3-D-LIFE); dimensions without a price get no
  crossover claim, only their measured per-query gap. Dimension-drop arms
  inherit donor pretraining compute stated-not-netted (§1.3a); Sardana gives
  the principled reason this matters: the donor was already sized for
  someone's inference demand, not ours.

## 10. What beat matched baselines, under what accounting (the skeptical cut)

The bead's standing question, answered for the SYS scope [STIPULATED
synthesis; constituent figures LIT-BACKED / MEASURED as tagged]:

1. **Systems wins in this literature are accounting wins.** DistServe's 7.4×
   is a goodput win at fixed SLO and fixed hardware — same model, same
   accelerators, better scheduling; it never claims a FLOP reduction.
   Sardana's −17…−28% are compute-accounting results under an explicit
   inference-demand model — analytic + 47-model-empirical, no benchmark-arm
   comparison at all. Neither is a "smarter model" claim, and both would
   evaporate under the wrong ledger (mean throughput; FLOP-only cost). The
   Programme-3 analogue: our f2b cost_ratio 0.103 [MEASURED:
   f2b-replicate.json, formal inputs only] is credible precisely because it
   was measured on a pinned rig, and the §1.3 resource vector is what keeps
   any future such claim honest.
2. **Unmatched-compute smells specific to the SYS scope**: (i) energy from
   TDP or utilisation models (killed by MLPerf Power, §2); (ii)
   latency-under-load percentiles taken from closed-loop drivers
   (coordinated omission, §5 — understates
   comparator tails if OUR driver is open-loop and theirs closed, i.e. this
   pitfall can bias FOR us; symmetric drivers are mandatory); (iii)
   warm-vs-cold asymmetry — quoting S warm (engine resident) vs a comparator
   cold (model load included), closed by §6's both-states rule; (iv) storage
   rent quoted while cold-start bytes and egress are dropped (closed by
   KOT-SIZE/2 figures (4)/(6)); (v) comparing against compute-optimal rather
   than overtrained baselines (§9 — closed by using SmolLM2-class anchors).
3. **Nothing in the verified literature licenses a claim that any
   neurosymbolic/store-bearing system beats a matched neural baseline on the
   FULL resource vector** — the vector methodology itself (MLPerf + power +
   goodput + lifecycle) has simply not been applied to that comparison at
   100M–2B anywhere I could find this session [carried as an absence-claim
   after search, not a proven negative]. Programme-3's KOT-COST/2 + KOT-LIFE/1
   would be, to my knowledge, the first pre-registered instance at this scale
   — which is a reason to expect referee scrutiny of the rig, and to
   over-document it [EXTRAPOLATION].

## 11. Open questions for Phase-1, and the hand-off

**Settled by this review** (Phase-1 may consume without re-litigating):

- MLPerf scenario/percentile/duration/caching machinery verified and reduced
  to an adoptable subset, with the loop semantics stated correctly:
  SingleStream/MultiStream closed-loop, Server open-loop Poisson; 600-s
  duration unconditional, early stopping adjusts query counts only (§1); the
  LLM TTFT/TPOT constraint TABLE verified, its binding percentile UNVERIFIED
  **teacher sampling density is the operative variable** (multiple chains per instance)
  [LIT: scotd-2023].
- Verifier-filtered bootstrapping needs no human rationales: STaR's
  keep-only-correct-answer loop lets 6B match a 30× larger finetuned model on
  CommonsenseQA [LIT: star-2022] — with a WEAK verifier (answer string match). A sound
  engine improves this filter's PRECISION on formal validity for covered statements; it is
  not a strict dominance — it costs coverage (only covered statements can be filtered) and
  remains exposed to formalization/specification error upstream of the check.
  [EXTRAPOLATION]
- Synthetic-curriculum existence proofs at our scale: phi-1 (1.3B, 7B curated/synthetic
  tokens, HumanEval 50.6%) with thin contamination controls — recipe inspiration, not
  evidence [LIT: phi1-2023]; TinyGSM (1.3B + 1.3B learned verifier → 81.5% GSM8K, verifier
  carries a large share) [LIT: tinygsm-2023; owned as analogy by PARSE.md].
- The programme's own asset alignment [STIPULATED]: AlphaGeometry's decisive ingredient was
  an **engine-generated synthetic corpus with exact traces** (100M theorems; ~10M with
  auxiliary constructions) [LIT: alphageometry-2024]. KOT possesses the RAW INGREDIENTS of
  the analog: a deterministic, seedable synthetic generator + µs engine over the covered
  grammar with controlled depth/polarity/paraphrase axes [MEASURED: encoder/ + poc/
  infrastructure exists; coverage ceiling per m0b 0.3542 friendliest-corpus remains the
  binding scope limit, feasibility-synthesis §0]. Whether the executor EXPOSES what the
  cited recipes consume — proof states, legal-action sets, step traces, compiler-style
  diagnostics — has not been demonstrated; the §8 oracle/API gate must show it before the
  AlphaGeometry analog is claimed. [STIPULATED gap]

## 5. Executor-in-the-loop training precedents (verifier-guided training)

Three rungs, by increasing integration depth — the evidence gets thinner as integration
gets deeper [STIPULATED framing]:

1. **Executor as data filter (expert iteration / rejection finetuning).** The
   best-evidenced rung: GPT-f value-function training from its own verified searches
   [LIT: gptf-2020]; compute-matched superiority over search-only [LIT:
   polu-curriculum-2022]; HTPS online training [LIT: htps-2022]; ReST-EM's binary-feedback
   EM loop (gains scale with size, diminish over iterations) [LIT: restem-2024]; STaR at
   6B [LIT: star-2022]; plateau caused by statement supply, fixed by self-play
   conjecturing [LIT: stp-2025].
2. **Executor as RL reward / feedback channel.** RLPAF (Lean pass/fail as reward)
   [LIT: dsprover15-2024]; CodeRL's unit-test critic at 770M-class scale [LIT:
   coderl-2022]; RLEF — training the model to CONSUME execution feedback across turns cuts
   inference samples by an order of magnitude at 8B/70B [LIT: rlef-2024]; Goedel-V2's
   compiler-feedback self-correction as an ablatable +2.3pt component [LIT: goedel-v2-2025];
   Logic-LM's solver-error self-refinement at inference [LIT: logic-lm-2023]. Directly
   licenses training the H-VL retry policy rather than only bolting retry on.
3. **Executor literally in the loss (differentiable).** Semantic loss (circuit-compiled
   constraints) [LIT: semantic-loss-2018]; blackbox-differentiation through exact solvers
   (Gurobi/Blossom V/Dijkstra as layers with informative gradients) [LIT:
   blackbox-solvers-2020]; DeepProbLog/Scallop (§2). All module-scale; **no published
   demonstration at ≥100M-LM scale with a logic engine in the loss was found in this
   search**. This rung has no precedent found, and no disproof — an absence at this
   review's search scope, not an established field fact.

**The scaling-flaws boundary** (re-anchored from RAG.md §4): the decay of verifier-guided
search at large budgets is a LEARNED-verifier pathology (misranking prunes valid paths)
[LIT: scaling-flaws-2025]. The cited sound-checker systems do not report it; they report
plateau, with statement supply identified as one mechanism [LIT: stp-2025] (§1.3 — not
established as the universal constraint). For KOT this separates the failure surfaces
Phase-1 must instrument: engine **coverage** (the sound analog of verifier failure),
generator **supply/diversity** (the analog of conjecturer collapse), and
**formalization/specification error** (outside both, untouched by checker soundness).

## 6. Transfer to a µs deterministic executor (the verifier-loop bet's prior art)

What the literature establishes, and exactly where KOT's premise goes beyond it:

- **Small proposer + deterministic engine beating larger neural-alone: established at
  system level.** AlphaGeometry (151M LM + DD/AR engine, 25/30 vs prior best 10; the
  oft-quoted GPT-4 0/30 comparison was not confirmed in my Nature fetch — UNVERIFIED)
  [LIT: alphageometry-2024], with the successor showing the neural side kept scaling
  without the symbolic engine capping the system (84% of 25 years of IMO geometry)
  [LIT: alphageometry2-2025]; ReProver-299M's premise-retrieval system over GPT-4 zero-shot
  (unmatched accounting noted) [LIT: leandojo-2023]; Thor's +18pt from adding hammers
  [LIT: thor-2022]. In every case the engine contributes soundness and/or exhaustive local
  deduction; the LM contributes exactly what the engine cannot enumerate (constructions,
  premise relevance, tactic priors).
- **Checker speed is never the tested variable in this literature.** Where per-check costs
  are visible they sit around ~10ms–s [EXTRAPOLATION: range assembled from the cited
  systems' setups, not established by a latency study — same caveat as §1.3]; systems
  compensate with parallelism (10,000 CPU workers [LIT: alphageometry-2024])
  and still win. A 5.29–7.82 µs/query engine [MEASURED: programme premise,
  programme-3 §2 PREMISE block] is ~10³–10⁵× cheaper per check than any executor in this
  literature. Two consequences [EXTRAPOLATION, both]:
  (a) every verified-training-loop precedent in §5 becomes cheaper in its checking leg —
  but since checking is not reported as the cost ceiling in any cited system (§1.3),
  **the µs property does NOT by itself improve the loop's economics**; proposer sampling
  still dominates. The honest claim is "removes a cost term", not "changes the regime",
  for training. Cheap checking is also not automatically cheap trace generation,
  conjecture generation, proof search, tokenization, storage, or training (next bullet).
  (b) the candidate novel regime is **per-token/inference-time integration**: at µs per
  check, the engine could be consulted inside the decode loop (H-RULE-CD's engine-derived
  continuation sets; H-VL's verify-retry with negligible verify overhead) at a cost no
  published executor could sustain. **This search found no precedent** for a semantic
  executor consulted per decoding step — the nearest cousins are grammar-mask automata
  (syntax-only; owned by the parsing report), execution-guided partial-program
  decoding/selection (owned by PARSE.md — an acknowledged adjacent precedent at
  program-fragment granularity), and KV-side memories (no execution). The claim is "no
  precedent found in this search", NOT "no precedent exists": P3-LR-RULE owns the
  executor-backed constrained-decoding search and must complete before any novelty claim
  is treated as settled. This is where the µs property may be a real differentiator, and
  it is an H-RULE/H-VL design question, not an H-GU one. [STIPULATED hand-off]
- **The engine as data factory is the underexploited transfer.** AlphaGeometry's synthetic
  corpus and Goedel-V2's scaffolded synthesis both use the deterministic side to
  MANUFACTURE the training distribution [LIT: alphageometry-2024, goedel-v2-2025]. A µs
  engine removes the ENGINE-EXECUTION term from trace-generation cost at any curriculum
  size — but generator sampling, search, trace serialization, deduplication, storage, and
  training-token processing all still scale with curriculum size and will dominate; the
  speed buys a real but PARTIAL training-side term, not "effectively free" generation.
  [EXTRAPOLATION]
- **What nothing in this literature licenses:** that any of this closes the NL boundary
  (all cited systems consume FORMAL inputs or curated problem statements — the l3a-parse /
  a5-nl FAILs stand untouched [MEASURED: registry/verdicts, via feasibility-synthesis §0]);
  and that verifier-loop gains are kernel-CONTENT-specific rather than
  correct-alignment-specific (the f2b confound; unresolved pending knull / f2b-transfer
  [MEASURED: registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license]).

## 7. Open questions for Phase-1 (numbered; each maps to a design bead)

- **Q1 (P3-D-GU).** Does the frontier prover recipe (engine-generated curriculum +
  verifier-filtered self-training + feedback-consumption training) hold BELOW 2B, where no
  published prover lives? The 8B>671B and 4B>671B results make "yes" plausible; nothing
  demonstrates it. The H-GU ≥3-point sweep is exactly the right instrument; power it for
  seed variance per [LIT: zhou-lengthgen-2024].
- **Q2 (P3-D-GU).** Does shortcut learning [LIT: paradox-2022] persist when the training
  distribution is ENGINE-CONTROLLED (exact traces, controlled rule-priors/branching)?
  I.e., is the paradox a data artifact or an architecture limit? Pre-register
  within-problem-space shift splits alongside depth splits.
- **Q3 (P3-D-GU, co P3-D-RULE).** Can Discrete-NAR-style state discretization [LIT:
  discrete-nar-2025] be imposed on a transformer host (not a GNN) with engine-emitted
  state supervision, and does its perfect-OOD property survive the transfer? (Bridges to
  H-RULE-HL's bottleneck, ordered LAST in the placement ladder per programme §3.3.)
- **Q4 (P3-D-RULE).** At µs check cost, is per-token engine consultation (continuation-set
  derivation mid-decode) latency-viable at p95 on our hardware, and does it beat the same
  engine used only as a post-hoc verifier at matched total budget? No prior art found in
  this search either way (§6b; P3-LR-RULE to confirm).
- **Q5 (P3-D-PS).** Does solver-trace emulation (LoGiPT-style, engine traces distilled INTO
  the parser/generator) beat parse→execute pipelines on the S2 dangerous-wrong metric, or
  does removing the executor at inference reintroduce unsound outputs? The literature shows
  accuracy parity is achievable; it does not report S2-style soundness accounting.
- **Q6 (P3-D-GU, feeds P3-D-SEAL).** What is our statement-supply curve? One documented
  sound-loop plateau mechanism is provable-statement exhaustion [LIT: stp-2025;
  §1.3 — one mechanism, not the established universal constraint]; the covered slice is
  currently 108 concepts / m0b 0.3542 [MEASURED: feasibility-synthesis §0]. A conjecturer
  analog (engine-validated statement synthesis over the world layer) needs a cost model
  before any H-GU RL variant is budgeted.

## 8. Phase-1 hand-off

**What this review settled** (for the record; [STIPULATED] unless tagged):

1. The verifier-loop bet's TRAINING-side precedent is real, replicated, and in one case
   same-compute by the paper's own claim [LIT: polu-curriculum-2022]; the other headline
   comparisons are component ablations or unmatched (§1.2); its economics precedent at ≤2B
   is absent — H-GU's sweep is the first test, not a replication.
2. Differentiable logic is DEPRIORITIZED as an architecture direction for Phase-1 (no
   at-scale matched win found in this search; grounding + cost failure modes) — a
   prioritization decision at this review's scope, not a field-wide closure — retained
   only as loss-shaping tools (semantic loss, blackbox-diff) for a LATE H-GU variant if
   ever.
3. Trace distillation without an executor or hard interface is expected to shortcut
   (three independent negative lines: paradox/PrOntoQA/Faith-and-Fate) — H-GU variants
   must keep the engine in the loop (filter/reward/state-supervision), and every eval
   needs within-space shift splits, multi-seed powering, and depth extrapolation.
4. The µs engine's speed is a candidate DECODE-TIME differentiator (per-token
   consultation: no precedent found in this search; P3-LR-RULE must complete the adjacent
   search before "unprecedented" is claimed) and a partial DATA-FACTORY asset (removes the
   engine-execution term from trace curricula; generation/serialization/storage/training
   costs remain), but NOT a training-loop-economics differentiator (checking is not
   reported as the ceiling in any cited system).
5. Proof-planning, not step deduction, is the neural deficit [LIT: prontoqa-2023] —
   division of labour should give search/planning to the deterministic side.
6. pass@k pinning + benchmark-quality audit (miniF2F-v2 lesson) go into every Phase-1
   prereg touching reasoning suites.

**Common prereg requirements for ALL THREE beads (added per external review
rev-NTP-20260711b; [STIPULATED]):**

- **Oracle/API gate (blocking, first):** demonstrate that the KOT executor can actually
  EMIT what each proposed arm consumes — proof states, legal-action/continuation sets,
  step traces, compiler-style diagnostics, and search transitions. µs checking does not
  imply any of these exist; no arm is budgeted until its interface is shown (§3, §4.3, §6).
- **Full resource-vector accounting for every ablation** (KOT-COST/2, programme-3
  ASM-0810): equal model size or equal pass@k does NOT license "matched"; no scalar
  diagnostic alone licenses "compute-matched".
- **Factorial separation** of synthetic curriculum, proof traces, checker filtering,
  retry feedback, and hard state interfaces — no bundled-recipe arms whose components
  cannot be attributed.
- **Formalization/specification-error accounting distinct from checker validity** — a
  sound check certifies the formal statement, not the intended one (§1.3; miniF2F-v2).
- **"No precedent found" language** for all novelty claims until P3-LR-RULE and
  P3-LR-PARSE complete their adjacent searches.

**Design beads to create (coordinator: bd-create; this bead writes no beads per
governance):**

- **P3-D-GU** [DESIGN, P2, blocked-by: P3-LR-TINY + this review + P3-LR-FUSE]. This
  review's co-input: stage the training variants in the evidence order (1) engine-generated
  synthetic curriculum + iterative SINGLE-STEP trace distillation (ProofWriter/LoGiPT

item.text: The design cleanly avoids the NL-boundary mistake at the probe level: every input is already formal, and it explicitly licenses no real-input/W1 claim. The weak point is downstream: it currently conflates “formal field continuation” with “decode-time token mask,” and it does not yet specify the boundary adapter that would make that leap testable without oracle addressing.

# FABLE INTERPRETATION — f2b-transfer STAGE-1 (mechanical: endorsement kill (d) did NOT fire; A = 0.978, one-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.961; instrument: VALID; experiment state: stage-1 passed → expected INCOMPLETE-DATA (record append + verdict-gen pending), stage 2 licensed)

- **Author:** Fable (chief architect), 2026-07-11. This is the FABLE interpretive reading of
  the f2b-transfer STAGE-1 adjudication result. The coordinator computed the mechanical
  result; I own the epistemic reading. This document changes NO frozen record, NO verdict
  object, NO log, NO analysis output, and NO registered assumption; it runs no experiment,
  no git, no bd, no kb-sync.
- **Sources (read at source, in full):** `registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json` (FROZEN,
  frozen_sha256 b341a090…, esp. H-TRANSFER/H-CIRC, kill_criterion_verbatim clause (d), and
  `extrapolation_envelope_verbatim` — BINDING on every line below),
  `poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json` (the coordinator's mechanical
  stage-1 result, restated verbatim, never recomputed), `poc/f2b-transfer/design.md`
  (prereg_doc, sha-pinned in the record; §2, §4 incl. correction-1 standards S1–S7, §7,
  §8, §10), and `docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md` (the capstone, 2026-07-11).
- **Epistemic discipline:** every load-bearing line carries its `[TAG]` on the same logical
  line. **MEASURED** restates a measured/computed fact strictly within this record's own
  envelope; **STIPULATED** marks framing and frozen-design rulings adopted verbatim;
  **DERIVED** marks a simple analytic computation performed in THIS document from MEASURED
  counts and disclosed as absent from every cited artifact; **EXTRAPOLATION** is
  direction-only and never load-bearing. No MEASURED number here is recomputed;
  every MEASURED figure is the coordinator's or the frozen record's, cited. The one
  DERIVED figure (the §0 worst-case robustness bound) is computed in this document and
  flagged as such.

---

## 0. The mechanical facts, restated inside the envelope

PREMISE: [MEASURED: poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json] Over the 360 blind d-qa-t items, judge-1 (a kernel-naive HUMAN) and judge-2 (the pre-declared cross-vendor fallback, GPT-5.5, temperature 0, pinned prompt, per protocol §4.1) returned concordant labels on 324/360 — raw judge-pair agreement 0.90 ≥ the 0.80 G-adj bar. Concordant escapes: ZERO (n_undecided = 0) — no item was concordantly judged CONTENT-UNDECIDED. Over the 324 resolved-by-concordance items, blind external gold equals the kernel's MEMBERSHIP gold on 317 → external endorsement A = 317/324 = 0.978, one-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.961.

PREMISE: [MEASURED: same source — the gate and the kill] `adjudication_valid` = TRUE on every pre-registered G-adj clause (n_adjudicated 360 ≥ 300; unresolved 36 = 10% ≤ 15%; agreement 0.90 ≥ 0.80) and `stage1_endorsement_fail` = FALSE: the Wilson LB 0.961 sits far above the 0.70 kill bar. Kill (d) — the pre-GPU FAIL evaluable from the stage-1 record alone — did NOT fire.

ROBUSTNESS NOTE (supplementary, NOT a premise — no conclusion below rests on it; the kill evaluation stands on the actual §0 counts alone): [DERIVED: analytic worst-case bound computed in THIS document from the §0 MEASURED counts (317, 360); this robustness computation appears in NO cited artifact — it is absent from stage1-analysis.json — and is disclosed as this document's own arithmetic] The 36 discordant judge-1/judge-2 pairs are currently UNRESOLVED (the judge-3 human tie-break has not yet run) and are counted per the frozen formula as excluded from A's denominator. The endorsement conclusion is ROBUST to their resolution: in the worst case (all 36 resolve against membership) A = 317/360 = 0.881, one-sided 95% Wilson LB ≈ 0.850 (this document's declared one-sided convention; the two-sided 95% lower endpoint would be ≈ 0.843) — still far above the 0.70 kill bar. No judge-3 outcome can fire kill (d).

PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json verdict_rules + design.md §7 — the frozen RULES restated verbatim; DERIVED: this document's application of those rules to the §0 facts — an EXPECTED state, not an issued one] With rule 1 (G-adj INSTRUMENT-INVALID) not matching and rule 2 (stage-1 FAIL) not matching, and every stage-2 field unset, the registered EXPECTED lawful state of the experiment is **INCOMPLETE-DATA** — stage-1 passed, fields for later rules unset, fail closed. This is the frozen record's own named intermediate state, and it is the state that LICENSES stage 2. It is NOT yet an issued experiment state: the formal stage-1 record append and mechanical verdict-gen have not run; INCOMPLETE-DATA is what verdict-gen is expected to emit when they do, per the frozen protocol order (design.md §4.5).

PREMISE: [MEASURED: m0b, restated mandatorily per the envelope] Kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, measured on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance — NOT general coverage. Every claim below is bounded to the kernel-covered slice.

---

## 1. What the stage-1 pass adjudicates: H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC

LOAD-BEARING (the central reading, resting on the §0 MEASURED facts read against the frozen hypothesis text): H-CIRC's sharpest, cheapest, pre-registered stage-1 prediction did NOT fire — the result strongly DISCONFIRMS the arbitrary-codebook/content-level BRANCH of H-CIRC, at this kernel instance and rung, on the kernel-covered kernel-rendered slice, and nothing wider. The frozen record gave H-CIRC a zero-GPU falsification route: if the kernel records are "an arbitrary-but-self-consistent codebook", blind judges who have never read them — answering from ordinary competence with the covered everyday concepts, with a mandatory cannot-say escape — should scatter or escape, driving A near option-chance/claim-base-rate and firing kill (d) before a dollar of GPU is spent [STIPULATED: H-CIRC verbatim, hypotheses[1] + design.md §2]. The observed result is not merely above the bar; it is near ceiling: A = 0.978 with Wilson LB 0.961 against a 0.70 kill bar, and the escape hatch H-CIRC needed — built in precisely so that "the kernel's content does not describe this concept" would be expressible — fired concordantly on ZERO of 360 items [MEASURED: §0]. The blind external dual-judge gold — human-anchored by judge-1 (kernel-naive, blind to provenance, membership gold, and record structure), with every resolved label conditional on concordance from the pre-declared GPT-5.5 judge-2 — reproduced the kernel's own gold labels on essentially the entire resolved set. Reconciling an arbitrary-codebook story with that contact is, on this slice, very hard [INTERPRETIVE INFERENCE from the §0 MEASURED facts read against the design's own §2 contrast — an inference of this document, NOT a stipulated fact of the frozen record, and NOT a refutation of H-CIRC as frozen: see the next paragraph].

LOAD-BEARING (the symmetric honesty): H-CIRC is NOT refuted, because the frozen hypothesis is disjunctive — "A near chance … **and/or** no external-gold lift (kill a)" [STIPULATED: hypotheses[1] verbatim]. Stage 1 strongly disconfirms the CONTENT-level circularity branch (the codebook story) at this instance and slice; it rejects H-CIRC's low-endorsement leg and removes the gold-definition leg, and does no more. The SYSTEM-level residual — whether the verifier-retry LIFT survives re-scoring against this externally-fixed gold — is exactly what the stage-2 GPU arms exist to measure, and kills (a), (b), (c) all remain live; H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC is fully adjudicated only by the primary and its controls. The design's own decision table (design.md §10, row "A high, primary fails") pre-names the outcome where content is externally endorsed but the system lift does not transfer; nothing in stage 1 forecloses it [STIPULATED: design.md §10; §2 layer-2 "measure, don't extrapolate"].

LOAD-BEARING (what stage 1 is evidence FOR): H-TRANSFER's content premise — "the kernel records carry semantic content that is true of the covered concepts as externally understood" — now has direct, blind, human-anchored measured support at this instance, on this slice [MEASURED: A = 0.978 within the envelope; STIPULATED: the H-TRANSFER wording, design.md §1]. Every one of the 317 agreements carries a kernel-naive HUMAN's blind label (concordance construction: the resolved label IS judge-1's label wherever it counts) — but inclusion in the resolved set is conditional on concordance with judge-2 (GPT-5.5), so the gold AUTHORITY is the frozen dual-judge procedure, human-anchored by judge-1, not the human alone. Even so stated, this is categorically stronger than the f2b-transfer-llmproxy stand-in (A_1p = 0.95, single LLM judge family, weak proxy per ASM-0022): the proxy's letter-level circularity break is now a human-anchored one [MEASURED: stage1-analysis.json; capstone §2 for the proxy's status; the numeric proxy-vs-human differencing is left to the coordinator against the pinned comparator — direction-consistency only is noted here, EXTRAPOLATION].

INTERPRETIVE NOTE (direction-only, never a premise): with A = 0.978, external and membership gold nearly coincide on the labelled set, which makes a LARGE dual-scoring gap (lift_mem ≫ lift_ext) arithmetically unlikely on the stage-2 eval prefix — but the frozen design explicitly forbids inferring the primary from A ("retry dynamics on the judge–kernel disagreement set are not a pure function of A"; promotion requires the measured system-level result) [EXTRAPOLATION, bounded by STIPULATED: design.md §2 layer 2]. Stage 2 is cheap (~$1 point estimate, caps $15/4 GPU-h) and is the only lawful way to convert this endorsement into a transfer claim [STIPULATED: design.md §9].

## 2. What a stage-1 endorsement pass LICENSES — and what it does NOT

**Licensed, exactly this and no more:**

- LOAD-BEARING: it removes the gold-definition-circularity confound — the specific residual f2b-replicate's assessment foregrounded ("content-specificity ≠ ground-truth independence") — for THIS kernel instance (kernel-v0/molecules-v0 as pinned) at this rung of item surface: kernel-covered, kernel-RENDERED templated definitional QA over the 108 covered concepts, under THIS gold standard (blind dual-judge adjudication per the pinned d-adj-t protocol, judge sourcing disclosed: human judge-1 + pre-declared GPT-5.5 fallback judge-2) [MEASURED: A/LB within extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; STIPULATED: envelope clause "A PASS removes exactly ONE confound … gold defined by the kernel's own string-equality loses its gold-definition leg"]. There now exists an external gold authority — the frozen blind dual-judge procedure, human-anchored by judge-1 (a kernel-naive human) with pre-declared GPT-5.5 concordance required for every resolved label — and the kernel's membership gold agrees with it at 0.978 on this slice. "Gold := whatever the kernel says" is no longer the only reading of the F2-line gold.
- LOAD-BEARING: it licenses STAGE 2 — the expected lawful INCOMPLETE-DATA state permits the GPU arms to run under the frozen stage discipline (judge-3 tie-break → d-adj-t pin → record append + verdict-gen → reuse-check → dry-plan → mock → single GPU run) [MEASURED: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json stage_discipline + verdict_rules (INCOMPLETE-DATA the lawful intermediate state licensing stage 2, restated verbatim); DERIVED: the state itself is EXPECTED, formal append + verdict-gen pending per §0; design.md §7/§11 steps 4–6].
- It retires, for this design, the cheapest FAIL route: the experiment can no longer end at ~$0; its remaining outcomes are decided by the stage-2 arms [MEASURED: kill (d) evaluable-from-stage-1-alone, not fired; robustness per §0's DERIVED note].

**NOT licensed — each item within the binding envelope, restated without widening [STIPULATED: extrapolation_envelope_verbatim, clause by clause]:**

- It does NOT establish the stage-2 verifier-offload lift on external gold. The primary (acc_ext(135M+kernel-verify-retry, k=4) − acc_ext(135M-alone)), the shuffled-content control (kill b), the gloss-commodity control (kill c), the non-inferiority-vs-1.7B headline, and every instrument gate on the GPU arms (engagement, headroom, separation, P10) are UNRUN. No verifier-offload promotion beyond the self-authored slice is licensed by A, however high [STIPULATED: design.md §2 layer 2; the envelope binds any PASS, and there is no PASS yet — the mechanical state is INCOMPLETE-DATA].
- It does NOT license external question-STYLE ecological validity. The item surface stays kernel-rendered templated QA; public-benchmark surfaces remain unmeasured for system lift, and measurably so — the deterministic verifier cannot engage them (f2b-replicate d-ext: verify/alone byte-identical at every seed) [MEASURED: the record's own d-ext assumption; STIPULATED: envelope]. The constrained IF-C answer surface remains and stays disclosed.
- It does NOT license any coverage-general claim: coverage 0.3542 at molecules-v0, one incomplete kernel-v0 instance, restated mandatorily [MEASURED: m0b; STIPULATED: envelope].
- It does NOT license any benchmark-style, PRM-class, or scale claim: HC3 stays indexed to the 1.5B class of f2b-replicate; two host rungs license a SIGN, not a slope; nothing beyond ≤1.7B hosts and the fixed k=4 budget [STIPULATED: envelope].
- It does NOT bear on the NL/reachability boundary at all: no natural-language input, no parser, and no host model appears anywhere in stage 1. The l3a-parse/a5-nl FAILs stand untouched; a deployment path from natural input to this formal slice remains negative at scope [MEASURED: capstone §1 C2, each figure index-bound; STIPULATED: this framing].

**Instrument honesty carried with the result:**

- Judge-2 was the pre-declared LLM fallback, not a second human. This was frozen in advance with E5 leak-checked-judge discipline and must be disclosed in every readout; the human anchor of the result is judge-1, present in every resolved label [STIPULATED: protocol §4.1; MEASURED: sourcing as recorded].
- The correction-1 adjudication standards (S1/S4/S6) RAISE A by removing minimality- and style-driven escapes, with a frozen argument that no H-CIRC signal is lost (under H-CIRC the clauses are false/misfitting and the disagreeing label still forces through) and with the A-direction of every rule disclosed pre-data [STIPULATED: design.md §4.7, adopted verbatim — a pre-data clarification, not a post-hoc patch; the two accepted honest costs (contentless segments lose credit; genuinely-shared donor content counts against, ~1% order) both push A DOWN, and A still reads 0.978].
- The 36 discordant pairs (10%) are within the 15% instrument cap and cannot move the verdict [MEASURED: §0]. GOVERNANCE NOTE (sequencing, not a contradiction of any number): the frozen protocol (§4.4–4.5) runs the judge-3 tie-break BEFORE d-adj-t is hash-pinned and the formal stage-1 record appended, and the stage-2 eval set is the deterministic 250-prefix of the externally-labelled items — judge-3's resolutions can add labels and therefore fix the prefix composition. The ~15-minute judge-3 round should complete before the d-adj-t pin; the epistemic reading above is invariant to its outcome (worst-case A = 0.881), but the formal record append should follow the frozen order [STIPULATED: protocol §4.5; recommendation to the coordinator/runner, no record modified here].

## 3. CAPSTONE DELTA — recommendation only (do NOT edit feasibility-synthesis.md here)

DECISION (recommendation only, applied by the coordinator or a later synthesis pass): the capstone (`docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md`, 2026-07-11) should incorporate the stage-1 result as follows [STIPULATED: consistent with the capstone's maintenance discipline under ASM-0760/0761]:

1. **Situate it precisely: the FIRST strong measured POSITIVE crossing into ground-truth-INDEPENDENT gold — on the gold-authority axis, not the input axis.** The capstone's governing boundary line ("every MEASURED crossing of a boundary into real or natural INPUT either FAILs or is INCONCLUSIVE", ASM-0762) SURVIVES VERBATIM: stage 1 crossed a different boundary — who fixes the gold — while the input surface stayed kernel-rendered. Add a distinct, disclaimed companion line, not a revision of ASM-0762: *the first time the kernel's own content was scored against an authority it did not define — blind external dual-judge gold, human-anchored by judge-1 — it was endorsed at A = 0.978 (Wilson LB 0.961, n=324 resolved of 360; robust worst-case 0.881 pending judge-3)*. This is the precise contrast with the NL-boundary FAILs: those measured whether outside input can REACH the kernel (no, at scope); this measured whether the kernel's content is externally ENDORSED under this frozen protocol when reached (yes, at 0.978, on this slice). One axis negative, the other now positive; neither reads onto the other [EXTRAPOLATION: the axis contrast, direction-only, never a premise].
2. **§2 efficiency — update the f2b-replicate confound paragraph.** The LOAD-BEARING line "the only experiments that could settle it — the human f2b-transfer Stage-1, or the K-NULL ablation — are unrun" is now stale: the human Stage-1 HAS read out, positively, at the content level. Replace with: the gold-definition-circularity leg of the f2b-replicate confound is REMOVED at this kernel instance (stage-1 A = 0.978, LB 0.961, human-anchored, kill (d) not fired); the remaining opens are (i) the SYSTEM-level transfer of the lift on external gold — the stage-2 GPU arms, now the cheapest verdict-moving compute in the registry (~$1 point estimate, $15 cap, no longer human-blocked) — and (ii) the alignment-vs-content attribution (knull), which stage 1 does NOT touch: a blind judge endorsing the records' truth says nothing about whether a generic aligned answer-key would produce the same lift.
3. **§3 ledger — split the f2b-transfer row.** Move "f2b-transfer (human judge-1) | FROZEN-UNRUN, human-blocked" to: stage-1 adjudication DONE (endorsement PASS, A 0.978/LB 0.961, adjudication_valid TRUE, mechanical state INCOMPLETE-DATA; judge-3 tie-break ~15 min pending, verdict-robust); stage-2 GPU arms PENDING, with the mandatory registered tail still ahead — judge-3 tie-break (~15 min human, verdict-robust), d-adj-t hash-pin, formal stage-1 record append + verdict-gen, THEN the ~$1 GPU run; none of these is human-annotation-scale, but none is optional, so the honest label is "cheap-mechanical-steps + compute", not "compute-blocked only". Reversal power stays "Verdict-moving" — but the mover is now stage 2, and its blocking constraint has flipped from human annotation to that short mechanical tail plus a trivial GPU run. The capstone's bottom-line claim "the binding constraint on both verdicts is not compute — it is human annotation" needs a carve-out for this line: its human constraint is now nearly discharged (only the ~15-minute judge-3 round remains human).
4. **The efficiency verdict does NOT move off INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING — and must not, yet.** Four reasons, each frozen: (i) the mechanical state is INCOMPLETE-DATA, not PASS — no end-task number on external gold exists; §0's governing sentence ("ZERO audited end-task wins … attributable to kernel content") stands verbatim, since stage 1 is not an end-task win (no host model, no task); (ii) the capstone's pending leg (a) was disjunctive across TWO confounds and stage 1 discharges only the gold-circularity half — knull (content vs generic-aligned-key) is untouched and unrun; (iii) legs (b) mint-cost (A-F0, key-gated) and (c) consumption channel (A-E2) are untouched; (iv) the envelope forbids reading A as a transfer result. What DOES change is the verdict's TEXTURE and its expected resolution path: H-CIRC lost its cheapest support and its zero-GPU kill route; the standing INCONCLUSIVE is now one short, cheap, pre-registered GPU run (plus the mandatory judge-3/pin/append tail) away from its registered adjudication. That outcome space is NOT binary: the frozen verdict rules admit the programme's first ground-truth-independent end-task PASS (primary and controls hold), a sharp well-scoped FAIL — kill (a): content endorsed, system lift doesn't transfer; or kills (b)/(c), which fail the experiment on control grounds (shuffled-content / gloss-commodity) WITHOUT cleanly adjudicating the residual system-level H-CIRC leg — and also INSTRUMENT-INVALID (GPU-arm gates), NULL, or INCONCLUSIVE [STIPULATED: verdict_rules + design.md §10]. The honest capstone phrasing: *"the efficiency thesis's decisive experiment is no longer human-blocked; its content premise just survived its first blind human contact at 0.978"* [EXTRAPOLATION: direction-only, never a premise].
5. **Correctness thesis — direction-only colour, NO verdict movement.** Stage 1 is registered on the efficiency line, but it is also the first blind human-anchored (dual-judge) endorsement of kernel explication content at the whole-item level (S1 fit+identification, S4 generic standard) — a positive datum on the same content-validity flank where g3-llmproxy-v3 posted a clause-literal proxy WARNING (see that record's own readout; it is outside this document's declared source list, so this is a pointer, not a restated figure). These do not contradict: different instruments, different grain (whole-explication endorsement vs clause-wise literal necessity), one human-anchored and one LLM-proxy. The remaining human step on THIS line is only the judge-3 discordance tie-break (36 items) — stage 2 is a GPU run, not a human adjudicator; the full human adjudication on the g3 line is GATE-H. Recommend recording the pair as the two current, non-contradictory readings of the content-validity axis, neither reading onto the other's endpoint [EXTRAPOLATION: direction-only; the correctness verdict's two decisive legs (g2, NL parser) are untouched and it stays INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING].

   177	NTP.md §8 requires demonstrating the executor "can actually EMIT what each
   178	proposed arm consumes — proof states, legal-action/continuation sets, step
   179	traces, compiler-style diagnostics, and search transitions."

   181	| §8 item | today | grade |
   182	|---|---|---|
   183	| legal-action / continuation sets | no API; exact sets derivable externally at measured µs–ms cost | **ABSENT as emission; substrate shown** |
   184	| step traces (accepts) | provenance+license certificate; closed for 77.3% (l3a) / 51.2% (a5) of covered answers | **PARTIAL** |
   185	| step traces (rejects) | code + prose only; nothing replayable | **ABSENT** |
   186	| compiler-style diagnostics | closed ERR_* code set (machine-matchable, the a5 control families score on exact codes) but no structured operands (no step, no violation refs, no expected/got) | **PARTIAL** |
   187	| proof states / search transitions | the v0 engine is a depth-1 deterministic lookup oracle with a pre-registered validation order — there IS no multi-step search; its complete derivation is the validation-step + consulted-records sequence, which B1 emits. Anything deeper (subClassOf closure) is refused at load by design (`ERR_AXIOM_UNIMPLEMENTED`) | **collapses to B1; deeper states out of scope** (ASM-0980) |

   191	  NTP.md (§3, §4.3) therefore remain STIPULATED gaps and MUST NOT be cited
   192	  as available capability until §5's build passes its acceptance tests

   196	## 4. Why this is the right bar (and not a stricter one)
   197	
   198	- PREMISE: the dependent arms consume, at minimum: admissible-token sets
   199	  (P3 masks), self-contained derivations for curricula and audit (P6, H-VL),
   200	  and machine-readable failure operands for retry training (H-VL, RLEF-shape
   201	  feedback) [STIPULATED: ASM-0980; the arm-side requirements restate
   202	  round1-critique-synthesis A.8/A.9 and NTP.md §5–6].
   203	- A stricter bar (theorem-prover-grade proof objects, search-tree
   204	  transitions) tests capability no registered arm consumes and the v0 engine
   205	  was never specified to have; a weaker bar ("the engine answers and
   206	  refuses deterministically") is what l3a/a5 already measured and is exactly
   207	  the assumption NTP.md flagged as insufficient. Replay-closure is the
   208	  weakest property that makes a trace *checkable by reconstruction* — the
   209	  property distillation curricula and audit both need.
   210	
   211	## 5. The build: what closes the gate, priced [STIPULATED: ASM-0982]
   212	
   213	Three additive, opt-in, CPU-only engine extensions; the default `query()`
   214	emission stays byte-identical (the frozen l3a/a5 instruments consume it):
   215	
   216	- **B1 — trace emission** (`query(q, trace=True)`): ordered validation-step
   217	  list; per step the outcome and EVERY consulted record reference — axiom
   218	  constraint refs, world-record ids *including licensing-support class
   219	  assertions* (§2.2 items 1–2), canonicalisation flips (§2.4), definitional
   220	  provenance + consulted mint-bridge entries (§2.2 item 4). Emitted for
   221	  accepts AND rejects. ~150–250 lines + tests.
   222	- **B2 — continuation API** (`continuations(partial)`): the ASM-0980 G1/G2
   223	  sets, derived analytically from the engine's existing indexes (`rel_fwd`
   224	  keys, `classes`, `defn`, licensing sets) rather than by query loops; the
   225	  probe's brute-force adapter is the frozen reference semantics, equivalence-
   226	  tested. ~120–200 lines + tests.
   227	- **B3 — structured refusal diagnostics**: refusals gain machine operands —
   228	  terminating step, and for `ERR_CONFLICT` stable references into the
   229	  store-violation objects (§2.4). ~60–100 lines.
   230	
   231	Acceptance, all mechanical, all required (ASM-0982): (a) replay closure
   232	100% on both pinned eval sets consuming trace-named records (baseline:
   233	464/600, 438/855); (b) B2 ≡ reference adapter on all worked partials;
   234	(c) byte-identical default emissions pre/post (both frozen instruments);
   235	(d) doubled-pass determinism on all new surfaces. Estimated total ~330–550
   236	added lines, zero GPU, ≤2 focused agent-days. Then re-run the gate as the
   237	registered P3-E-NTP-GATE-0 (ASM-0981) — its instrument is `probe.py`
   238	re-pointed at trace-named record sets.

   315	### 1.3 Inference cost — KOT-COST/2 (the resource vector) + the neural-FLOP diagnostic
   316	
   317	The review's technical error #7 is accepted: a ledger in which symbolic
   318	computation enters at zero is not a compute measure — a system doing substantial
   319	CPU search, retrieval, decompression, or I/O could look "compute-matched" by
   320	definition. Revision 1's L1 is therefore RENAMED and DEMOTED to what it actually
   321	is, and the binding ledger becomes a full resource vector [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   322	
   323	- DECISION: inference cost is the measured **resource vector KOT-COST/2**, per
   324	  query and per suite, on pinned hardware under the P3-D-HW repeatability
   325	  protocol: (a) accelerator time + estimated accelerator ops; (b) CPU-seconds;
   326	  (c) bytes read from storage and network; (d) energy (RAPL on CPU,
   327	  NVML/`nvidia-smi`-class counters on GPU, whole-run integration — not query-level
   328	  spot reads); (e) peak accelerator memory AND host RSS; (f) end-to-end latency at
   329	  p50 and p95; (g) throughput under a pinned concurrency distribution; (h) warm-
   330	  and cold-cache conditions both measured, including startup/index-load time;
   331	  (i) time-to-first-token and inter-token latency for generative arms; (j) output
   332	  length controlled (pinned max-token + stop discipline) or cost normalised per
   333	  emitted token, pinned per benchmark. Methodology borrows MLPerf inference +
   334	  power measurement discipline (REVIEW-CITED: MLPerf inference-datacenter
   335	  benchmarks + MLPerf power methodology; verified and operationalised by
   336	  P3-LR-SYS → P3-D-HW before the framework freezes) [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   337	- DECISION: the analytic neural-FLOP ledger survives as the **neural-FLOP
   338	  diagnostic** (2·params·tokens + attention terms, pinned formula sheet): useful
   339	  to keep the neural parts honest and hardware-independent, REPORTED always,
   340	  BINDING never — no "compute-matched" claim may cite it alone [STIPULATED:
   341	  ASM-0810; corrects technical error #7].

   854	## 4. The gate ladder (information-gaining gates × compute rungs)
   855	
   856	Revision 1 organised progression by compute rung alone, which the review
   857	correctly noted lets an architecture fail ambiguously (graph encoder? retrieval?
   858	NL parse?). Revision 2 organises every family's progression around five
   859	**information-gaining gates**, run cheapest-first, so a failure LOCALISES
   860	[STIPULATED: ASM-0817]:
   861	
   862	| Gate | Question | Input regime | Cost class | What a FAIL localises to |
   863	|---|---|---|---|---|
   864	| **G1 coverage-ceiling** | Can PERFECT oracle use of the store move the index by δ? (`Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b κ_b (1−a_b^cov)`) | paper/CPU: census + baseline accuracies | ~$0, no GPU | coverage — kill without touching the mechanism |
   865	| **G2 oracle-interface** | With gold parses/subgraphs/record addresses, does the mechanism help at all? | ORACLE inputs; labelled `oracle-diagnostic`; NO W1 claim (ASM-0814) | cheap GPU | the mechanism itself (encoder, fusion, placement) |
   866	| **G3 boundary-degradation** | How much of the G2 effect survives realistic NL mapping? | natural benchmark text through the NLB-gated front-end; the P3-D-ORACLE decomposition attributes the G2→G3 loss (parse vs retrieval vs execution vs generation) | cheap-moderate | the NL front-end / retrieval addressing |
   867	| **G4 full-system-vs-frontier** | Does the whole system beat the resource-constrained frontier F(B_k)? (= a W1 attempt) | natural inputs, full framework, sealed eval | the rung's main spend | the competitive thesis at that rung |
   868	| **G5 scaling** | Does the W1 margin hold or improve across ≥2 sizes? | as G4, two rungs | gated spend | scale-transfer |
   869	
   870	Compute rungs remain as HOMES for the gates (they say where the compute comes
   871	from, not what is learned):
   872	
   873	| Rung | Params-equivalent (orientation only; the binding constraint is the budget vector B_k) | Compute home | Est. cost |
   874	|---|---|---|---|
   875	| **R-0** | 1–30M, TinyStories-class data | Local box + single free GPU (Modal/ARC) | ~$0 beyond free pool |
   876	| **R-1** | 100–200M (SmolLM2-135M anchor) — the decisive cheap rung: every family's G1–G3 and first G4 | Oxford ARC / Modal academics; single-GPU jobs | ≤ ~50 GPU-h/family |
   877	| **R-2** | 300–500M (SmolLM2-360M anchor) — G4 for R-1 survivors; the W2 sweep iff W1 passes here | ARC/Modal; AIRR Gateway if awarded | ~100–500 GPU-h total |
   878	| **R-3** | 1–2B (SmolLM2-1.7B anchor) — G4/G5 at a size the outside world cares about; index version bump | AIRR Gateway 10k GPU-h class; TRC | gated: maintainer + secured allocation |
   879	| **R-4** | 3–8B | second-wave grants (§6) | gated: explicit maintainer fork |
   880	
   881	Rung/gate discipline: promotion requires the pre-registered primary of the
   882	current gate met; a family failing the SAME gate at two consecutive rungs (or
   883	failing G1/G2 outright, which are rung-independent) is closed at scope;
   884	architectures may change between rungs — every change re-enters through
   885	prereg-freeze; no silent drift. **Sequencing exemptions (per the review):**
   886	P3-E-NLB-1 and all coverage censuses/G1 computations do NOT wait for index
   887	calibration (P3-E-CAL) — they make no index claim and gate everything else, so
   888	they run immediately; every G4 (W1-relevant) experiment DOES require P3-E-CAL
   889	PASS plus the frozen KOT-FAIR/2 [STIPULATED: ASM-0817].

item.text:
## Verdict

The measured diagnosis is credible and useful; the build specification is not yet strong enough to freeze as the gate-clearing contract.

It is cheap and bounded as an engine-engineering task, but the proposed acceptance test can pass without demonstrating complete traces, reject replay, or deployable token-level continuation masks. I would revise before committing as the Phase-1 design. A B1/B3 implementation spike is reasonable now; no trace-, distillation-, or mask-consuming model experiment should start yet.

### 1. Concrete, buildable, cheap?

Mostly.

- B1 trace instrumentation and B3 structured diagnostics are plausibly a CPU-only, days-scale change.
- B2 is bounded only for the stipulated field-order/formal-query interface. The design demonstrates seven worked partials, with only one code-oracle operation, not a complete continuation contract ([probe.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/ntp-gate/probe.py:386)).
- The 330–550 LOC / two-day estimate is unsupported. Exact analytical G2 sets need semantics for every operation, legal partial, refusal, code-oracle desugaring, invalid prefix, and output mode. Returning 72,839 definition values also has nontrivial allocation and serialization cost.
- The 100M–2B band is largely irrelevant to this gate: no model runs. It establishes a possible engine interface, not that the interface can be integrated cheaply with a 100M–2B tokenizer/decode loop.
- “Public attributes” is informal, not a stable API contract; these are mutable Python fields rather than a versioned executor interface.

So: buildable cheaply, yes; priced accurately and integration-ready, no.

### 2. Falsifiable, decisive, NL-wall-safe?

The current-state finding is decisive: there is no explicit API, accept certificates replay only partially, and refusals have no certificate. The hash matches the stated artifact, and the failures are systematic.

The proposed build PASS is not yet decisive:

- Acceptance tests only covered answers. `replay_leg` explicitly skips refusals and non-covered records ([probe.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/ntp-gate/probe.py:136)), although B1 promises traces for accepts and rejects.
- B2 equivalence is required only on the seven hand-selected partials, not exhaustively over the finite grammar.
- Replay tests sufficiency—“this subset reproduces the answer”—not the stronger claim that every consulted dependency was emitted. A trace containing the whole store could pass.
- Replay uses the same engine implementation, so it tests internal self-consistency, not an independently checkable proof object. That is acceptable for an API gate, but not enough for the stronger audit/proof language currently used.

The NL-wall discipline itself is good. The design explicitly restricts this to formal `kot-query/1` inputs and licenses no real-input/W1 claim ([NTP-GATE.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md:245)), consistent with Programme-3’s oracle-diagnostic rule ([programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:194)). The f2b-transfer Stage-1 endorsement does not change this: it strengthens external truth endorsement on a covered, kernel-rendered slice, not NL reachability or system-level transfer.

There is no smuggled NL oracle inside this probe. But a field-level G2 set is not yet a token-level mask. Any downstream H-RULE-CD test must separately specify:

- how a natural/text prefix becomes a formal partial query;
- URN/value-to-token alignment;
- ambiguity and abstention;
- whether gold field boundaries or record addresses are supplied.

Without that, only an explicitly labelled oracle-diagnostic G2 test is lawful.

### 3. Technical errors and gaps

1. **“Exactly the records it names” is false in the replay implementation.** License references contain `#constraint`, but replay strips the suffix and reloads the entire axiom file ([probe.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/ntp-gate/probe.py:118)). Several files contain multiple constraints. The reported 77.3%/51.2% closure is therefore file-granular, not record/constraint-granular, and may contain false-positive replays.

2. **Reject traces have no replay semantics or acceptance endpoint.** Conflict refusals can carry a positive violation witness, but `ERR_NO_RECORD`, unknown-entity and similar conclusions depend on a complete store/index boundary. A restricted-record replay cannot prove absence. Specify either a corpus/index snapshot digest plus terminating-step evidence, or separate positive-witness and snapshot-relative rejection contracts.

3. **“Every consulted record” is undefined given global store construction.** Engine initialization scans axioms/world data, builds indexes and computes global CWA violations. Query-local witness records must be distinguished from build-time/global dependencies. Otherwise a literal complete trace may be enormous.

4. **The gate cannot clear all NTP §8 consumers.** B1–B3 can clear lookup-derivation, diagnostics and formal-field-continuation interfaces. They cannot clear proof states or search transitions for planning/search curricula because this executor has no such process. Calling those requirements a collapse into B1 changes the gate instead of satisfying it. Mark them `NOT APPLICABLE to v0 arms / NOT CLEARED for search-consuming arms`.

5. **Continuation coverage is insufficient.** There is no exhaustive finite-domain test over all legal prefixes, no invalid-prefix contract, no `define` candidate continuation, and only `callers-of` is exercised through the code wrapper.

6. **API absence is over-described as mechanised inspection.** It is a name-pattern search over `Engine` and function definitions in `kot_axiom.py`; it does not inspect the code-oracle surface comprehensively and cannot prove absence under arbitrary naming.

7. **The trace schema is underspecified.** It needs schema/version, store snapshot digest, stable constraint/world/bridge identifiers, ordered step enum, outcome vocabulary, canonicalisation metadata, and compatibility behavior.

### 4. KOT-FAIR/2 and resource accounting

The gate itself is correctly outside W1: it is a cheap interface diagnostic and need not wait for full KOT-FAIR/2 calibration.

However, it is not fully slotted into the programme:

- `P3-E-NTP-GATE-0` appears in steering but is absent from the formal Phase-1/Phase-2 tables and blocking graph ([programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:928)).
- It should block the relevant portions of P3-D-RULE, P3-D-GU and any diagnostic-consuming H-VL design—not necessarily each entire family.
- Downstream use must charge B2 index bytes, index construction/startup, CPU-seconds, host RSS, serialization, engine calls per decoded token, p50/p95 latency, throughput, and cold/warm behavior under KOT-COST/2 ([programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:323)).
- Trace-curriculum generation must also charge trace volume, serialization, deduplication, storage and lifecycle/index-building costs.
- Byte-identical default responses are a good compatibility test, but insufficient: eagerly built trace/continuation indexes could still change default startup time and RSS. Require lazy opt-in or byte/resource equivalence on the default path.

## Three most important concerns, ranked

1. **Gate-clearing acceptance is incomplete:** answers-only replay plus seven partials cannot clear reject traces, complete continuation semantics, or proof/search consumers.

2. **The replay measurement is technically misstated:** it reloads whole axiom files after stripping constraint IDs, so “exactly named records” and the closure percentages are not established at the claimed granularity.

3. **Formal continuations are being positioned too close to decode-time masks:** the tokenization/prefix/addressing boundary and its KOT-COST accounting remain entirely unbuilt; without them, only an oracle-diagnostic is licensed.

**Commit decision:** not sound to commit as the final gate-clearing Phase-1 design. Sound to commit after tightening the trace/reject contracts, making replay constraint-granular, expanding acceptance exhaustively, narrowing which NTP consumers the gate can clear, and adding the dependency/resource-accounting hooks. Safe to authorize the bounded engine implementation work after those edits; not safe yet to drive dependent model experiments.
